documents.text: Right faith, right knowledge, and right conduct constitute the path to liberation.
Right faith is belief in the true nature of reality (tattvas).
That faith is attained either by intuition (nisarga) or by acquisition of instruction (adhigama).
The categories of truth (tattvas) are: soul (jiva), non-soul (ajiva), influx of karma (asrava), bondage (bandha), stoppage of karma (samvara), shedding of karma (nirjara), and liberation (moksha).
These categories of truth are understood through instruments of knowledge (pramana) and points of view (naya).
Knowledge is of five kinds: sensory (mati), scriptural (shruta), clairvoyant (avadhi), telepathic (manahparyaya), and omniscient (kevala).
The first two kinds of knowledge are indirect (paroksha) as they require senses and mind.
The remaining three kinds of knowledge are direct (pratyaksha) as they are perceived by the soul directly.
Through sensory perception (mati-jnana) one cognizes through the five senses and the mind.
Scriptural knowledge (shruta-jnana) is preceded by sensory knowledge and is of two kinds: scriptural texts (anga-pravista) and non-scriptural texts (anga-bahya).
Clairvoyance (avadhi-jnana) is the direct cognition of material objects limited by space and time.
Telepathy (manahparyaya-jnana) is the direct cognition of the thoughts of others.
Omniscience (kevala-jnana) is the infinite, all-embracing direct knowledge of all reality.
The soul's characteristic is consciousness (upyoga).
Consciousness is of two kinds: knowledge (jnana) and perception (darshana).
The souls are of two types: mundane (samsari) and liberated (mukta).
Mundane souls are of two types: mobile (trasa) and immobile (sthavara).
Immobile souls are earth-bodied, water-bodied, fire-bodied, air-bodied, and plant-bodied.
Mobile souls have two, three, four, or five senses.
The senses are touch, taste, smell, sight, and hearing.
The non-soul categories are: medium of motion (dharma), medium of rest (adharma), space (akasha), matter (pudgala), and time (kala).
Matter (pudgala) possesses touch, taste, smell, and color.
Matter is found in the form of atoms (anu) and molecules (skandha).
The function of the medium of motion (dharma) is to support movement.
The function of the medium of rest (adharma) is to support rest.
The function of space (akasha) is to give place to all other substances.
The function of matter (pudgala) is to support pleasure, pain, life, and death.
The function of time (kala) is to support change, motion, duration, and succession.
The activities of body, speech, and mind constitute action (yoga).
This action (yoga) causes the influx (asrava) of karma.
Virtuous action leads to meritorious karma (punya), while wicked action leads to demeritorious karma (papa).

documents.sanskrit: Sakashayatvad-jiva karmano yogyan pudgalan-adatte sa bandhah.
documents.text: Passion (kashaya) causes the soul to absorb karmic matter, which binds the soul.
Bondage (bandha) is of four kinds according to: nature (prakriti), duration (sthiti), intensity (anubhaga), and quantity of space occupied (pradesha).
Non-violence is the path of peace; truth is the path of purity; non-stealing is the path of honesty.

documents.sanskrit: Sabbesim jiviyabhilaso, natthi koci maranabhilaso. Taha na haneyya na ghatayeyya.
documents.text: All living beings love life; they do not want to suffer or die. Therefore, do not kill or cause others to kill.

documents.sanskrit: Jaha mama dukkham appiyam, taha savva-jivanam pi. Evam janiya samatena pane na haneyya.
documents.text: Just as pain is undesirable to you, so is it to all other living beings. Recognizing this equality, practice compassion.

documents.sanskrit: Mana-visuddhi paramam samatarannam. Bhava-himsa savva-pavasya mulam.
documents.text: Violence in thought (bhava-himsa) is the root of all physical violence. Purify the mind first to attain true peace.

documents.sanskrit: Ahimsa paramo dharmo, ahimsa loka-nayika. Ahimsa savva-bhutanam rakkha-karaniyam.
documents.text: Ahimsa (non-violence) is the highest code of conduct. It is the protector of all living beings and the source of bliss.

documents.sanskrit: Yo pariggaham paricajai, so ahimsam palai. Parigraho bandha-mulam.
documents.text: One who is attached to material possessions (parigraha) cannot cultivate non-violence. Non-possession is freedom.

documents.sanskrit: Appano jayo jayo. Jo jayaei appanam, so jayaei savvam.
documents.text: He who conquers his own self conquers everything. Self-conquest is the greatest victory in the three worlds.

documents.sanskrit: Savva-bhutesu samada, esa dhammo sanantano. Samada-bhavana nirjara-karanam.
documents.text: Equanimity (samata) toward all beings is the essence of religion. It leads to the destruction of all karmic bonds.

documents.sanskrit: Saccam bhanehi madhuram, hitakarayam ca. Parahakaram saccam pi asaccam hoi.
documents.text: Truth (satya) must be spoken with sweetness and benefit. Harmful truth is equivalent to untruth.

documents.sanskrit: Adattadanam veramanam, asteyam dhammo. Jo na gayhai, so suhi.
documents.text: Do not take anything which is not given by its owner. Taking ungiven things constitutes theft (asteya).

documents.sanskrit: Bambhaceram paramam thavam. Tam pavananam rakkha-karanam.
documents.text: Celibacy (brahmacharya) is the supreme penance. It guards the energy of the body, speech, and mind.
The mind is like a wild elephant; control it with the hook of right knowledge and mindfulness.

documents.sanskrit: Kodo mano maya lobho, ca-kashaya jiva-malikaranayam.
documents.text: Anger, pride, deceit, and greed are the four passions (kashayas) that pollute the soul.

documents.sanskrit: Kodhala khamaya jine, manam maddavena. Mayam ajjavena jine, lobham tosa-guna.
documents.text: Conquer anger with forgiveness; conquer pride with humility; conquer deceit with simplicity; conquer greed with contentment.

documents.sanskrit: Khama virasya bhushanam. Khamemi savva-jive, savve jiva khamantu me.
documents.text: Forgiveness (kshama) is the ornament of the strong and the shield of the seeker. Forgive all and seek forgiveness from all.

documents.sanskrit: Mitri me savva-bhutesu, veram majjham na kenaci.
documents.text: I have friendship with all beings (mitri) and enmity toward none. This is the ultimate declaration of peace.
Karmic matter flows into the soul through channels of physical, mental, and vocal activities.

documents.sanskrit: Indriya-niyame samvaro. Samvarena jivo pavena na limpati.
documents.text: By controlling the senses and mind, the influx of karma is stopped (samvara). This is the key to spiritual progress.

documents.sanskrit: Tavo kamma-vana-dahano. Tapasa savva-dukkhanam antam hoi.
documents.text: Penance (tapa) is the fire that burns away the accumulated karma of countless lifetimes.

documents.sanskrit: Citta-sthirata dhyanam. Dhyanena jivo paramappanam havai.
documents.text: Meditation (dhyana) is the concentration of thought on a single object. It is the direct path to liberation.

documents.sanskrit: Appa karta ca bharta ca, duhkhasa ca sukhasa ca. Appa mittam-amittam ca.
documents.text: The soul is the master of its own destiny. No external god can give salvation or punishment.
Every soul has the potential to become a Paramatman (supreme soul) by destroying its karmic coverings.
Reality possesses three characteristics: origination (utpada), destruction (vyaya), and permanence (dhrauvya).
A substance (dravya) is that which has qualities (gunas) and modes (paryayas).

documents.sanskrit: Bahira-bhavas na sukkha-dukkha-karana. Atta-bhavo yeva karanam.
documents.text: External things are not the cause of happiness or misery. The cause lies within the soul's own passions.
Right faith is attained by associating with enlightened teachers, listening to scripture, and contemplation.
He who has right faith looks at the world with compassion and without prejudice.
Without right conduct, knowledge is like a lamp in the hands of a blind man.
Charity (dana) is the giving of wealth, food, knowledge, and protection to others with a pure heart.
The fourfold charity consists of: food (ahara), medicine (aushadha), knowledge (shastra), and fearlessness (abhaya).

documents.sanskrit: Bhava-shuddhi paramam dhyanam. Bhavena jivo mucchadi.
documents.text: Purity of mind (bhava-shuddhi) is the most important element of any spiritual practice.

documents.sanskrit: Lobho akanksha-rupo, toso paramam sukham. Trishna-vinasho mukti.
documents.text: He who is satisfied with what he has is the richest person. Greed is a bottomless pit.

documents.sanskrit: Para-ninda na kattavya, na parathanam thavam. Atta-guna-vibhushanam.
documents.text: Do not speak ill of anyone, nor praise yourself. Keep your mind focused on self-correction.

documents.sanskrit: Savve manuya samana, na koi jaccisa bhedah.
documents.text: The path of the Jinas is open to all, without distinction of caste, creed, or gender.

documents.sanskrit: Samayam goyam ma pamaye. Ayu-kkhayo divasa-ratti havai.
documents.text: Time is passing; do not delay for a single moment in practicing the path of liberation.

documents.sanskrit: Maranambhi bhayam natthi, jiva-nicchataya janiyo. Deho vinassadi, jivo na vinassadi.
documents.text: The wise man does not fear death, for death is only the shedding of an old garment by the soul.

documents.sanskrit: Namo Arihantanam, Namo Siddhanam, Namo Ayariyanam, Namo Uvajjhayanam, Namo Loe Savva Sahunam.
documents.text: Obeisance to the Arihants, the Siddhas, the Acharyas, the Upadhyayas, and all the Sadhus in the universe.

documents.sanskrit: Eso pancha namokaro, savva-papappanasano. Mangalanan ca savvesin, padhamam havai mangalam.
documents.text: These five obeisances (Navkar Mantra) destroy all sins and constitute the foremost auspicious mantra.
Liberation is the state of infinite knowledge, infinite perception, infinite power, and infinite bliss.
In the state of liberation, the soul resides at the top of the universe (Siddhashila), free from all karma forever.

documents.sanskrit: Sariro nauka-rupo, jivo naviko havai. Sansaro sagaro, tam taranayam appamado.
documents.text: The body is the boat, the soul is the sailor, and the samsara is the ocean. Cross the ocean with vigilance.

documents.sanskrit: Vinaya-mula jnana-prapti. Jo vinayi so suhi.
documents.text: Knowledge is obtained through humility (vinaya). The disciple who is humble attains the wisdom of the sages.
Humility is the root of all spiritual discipline. An arrogant student learns nothing.

documents.sanskrit: Jayam care jayam cittham jayamase jayam jaye; jayam bhunjanto bhasanto papam kammam na bandhati.
documents.text: The monk should walk carefully, stand carefully, sit carefully, lie down carefully, and eat carefully to practice non-violence.
Carefulness (samiti) is of five kinds: walking, speaking, begging, handling objects, and disposal of waste.
Restraint (gupti) is of three kinds: control of mind, control of speech, and control of body.
The householder should practice the small vows (anuvratas) in daily life to purify his conduct.
One should not consume food or drink after sunset (ratri-bhojana-virati) to protect subtle life forms.
Avoid unnecessary actions (anartha-danda) which cause injury to others without any benefit.
Daily meditation and self-reflection (samayika) for forty-eight minutes purifies the householder's mind.
Fast on auspicious days (posadhopavasa) to experience the life of a monk for a day.
Limiting travel and consumption (dik-vrata and bhogopabhoga-parimana) helps in practicing non-possession.
The final fast unto death (sallekhana) is practiced with equanimity when death is unavoidable.
Ahimsa is not just non-killing; it is active love, compassion, and service toward all beings.
Reality is complex. Avoid dogmatism (naya-vada) and respect the viewpoints of others.
The path of self-realization is difficult, like walking on the edge of a sword, but it is the only path to peace.
All worldly relationships are temporary (anitya). The soul is solitary (ekatva) in its journey.
The physical body is impure (ashuchi) and distinct from the pure soul. Cultivate detachment.
Karma is attracted by attachment (raga) and aversion (dvesha). Remove these dualities.
A pure soul has neither color, nor smell, nor taste, nor physical form. It is pure consciousness.
The universe is eternal, uncreated, and self-regulated by natural laws (loka-bhavana).
Human birth is extremely rare and precious. Use it to attain spiritual liberation.
True religion is not in external rituals, but in the purity of the soul.
The monk should bear all hardships (parishahas) like heat, cold, hunger, and insults with equanimity.
There are twenty-two hardships that a seeker must conquer on the path of self-control.
By internal penance like confession (prayaschitta), the soul sheds its karmic burdens.
Study of scriptures (svadhyaya) is the highest form of internal penance.
Serving the teachers and elderly seekers (vaiyavritya) destroys ego and builds humility.
Giving up all attachments (vyutsarga) is the final stage of internal penance.
The Siddhas are free from birth, aging, and death, residing in eternal bliss.
May the entire universe be blessed. May all beings be interested in each other's welfare.

documents.sanskrit: Dosha prayantu nasham, sarvatra sukhi bhavantu lokah.
documents.text: May all defects and sufferings vanish. May everyone experience peace and joy.
Obeisance to the Arihants (conquerors).
Right faith, right knowledge, and right conduct constitute the path to liberation.
